CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, username VARCHAR(50), password VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO users (id, username, password) VALUES (1, 'test_user', 'password123');
INSERT INTO users (id, username, password) VALUES (2, 'another_user', 'securepass');
SELECT USER, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) AS total_users;
SELECT u.username, (SELECT USER) FROM users u WHERE u.id = 1;
SELECT u.username FROM users u WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.var_values v WHERE v.value = USER);
SELECT (SELECT USER) AS current_user, u.* FROM users u WHERE u.id IN (SELECT id FROM users WHERE username LIKE 'test%');
SELECT USER FROM (SELECT username FROM users WHERE id = 2) AS subquery;
SET USER = 'another_user'; SELECT USER AS current_user, (SELECT u.username FROM users u WHERE u.id = 1) AS first_user;
CREATE TABLE roles (id INT PRIMARY KEY, role_name VARCHAR(50));
SELECT USER, r.role_name FROM roles r JOIN (SELECT USER AS current_user) AS cu ON r.role_name = 'admin' WHERE cu.current_user = 'test_user';
